skill.name: uiux-ops
skill.description: Operational guidance for planning, reviewing, and improving UI/UX design with consistent design-system practices.
skill.summary: Guides structured UI/UX design reviews, component decisions, accessibility checks, and documentation updates.
skill.author: A-Genno

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, weak cryptography, and high entropy indicators in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by front matter, Japanese prose, and inline file-name formatting, with no executable code or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backtick Formatting
low_findings.description: The static analyzer interpreted inline Markdown backticks around UIUX.md references as Ruby or shell execution. These lines are documentation references only and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are Markdown bullets that reference UIUX.md using inline code formatting. No shell syntax, command runner, script block, or executable instruction is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm Alert
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 3 as weak cryptography, but the line is only the skill description in YAML front matter. No cryptographic algorithm or security-sensitive implementation appears in the file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 contains descriptive Japanese text about UI/UX operations. There is no MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or similar weak algorithm usage.

low_findings.title: False Positive: High Entropy Heuristic
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged line 1 for high entropy, but the line is the standard YAML front matter delimiter. No binary, encrypted payload, encoded blob, or obfuscated content was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is short human-readable Markdown with YAML front matter and Japanese prose. Manual review found no hidden payload or obfuscated instructions.

content.value_statement: Teams often lose consistency when UI decisions, accessibility checks, and component rules are handled informally. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a concise operating guide for structured UI/UX review and documentation.
actual_capabilities: Summarizes core UI/UX principles for user-centered product design.
Guides design-system thinking for components, color, typography, and spacing.
Prompts accessibility review against WCAG-oriented expectations.
Supports responsive design checks across device and screen sizes.
Encourages documenting UI/UX decisions and changes in UIUX.md.
limitations: Does not include visual assets, templates, or component code.
Does not run automated accessibility or browser testing.
Does not define a full brand system or product-specific design tokens.
Requires project context to make detailed design recommendations.

use_cases.title: Review a Product Screen
use_cases.description: Check a screen for consistency, accessibility, responsive behavior, loading states, and error handling.

use_cases.title: Document UI Decisions
use_cases.description: Convert design choices and interaction changes into clear notes for UIUX.md.

use_cases.title: Align Design Reviews
use_cases.description: Use a shared checklist for component reuse, spacing, typography, and interaction feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Basic UI Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single screen or flow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this screen using the uiux-ops guidance. Focus on user goals, consistency, accessibility, responsive behavior, and clear interaction feedback.

prompt_templates.title: Design System Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review before implementing or refactoring UI components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate this interface against design-system principles. Identify reusable components, color issues, typography gaps, spacing inconsistencies, and documentation updates.

prompt_templates.title: Interaction Quality Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of states and behavior.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit the interaction design for feedback, error handling, loading states, and microinteractions. Recommend concrete improvements and note what should be recorded in UIUX.md.

prompt_templates.title: End-to-End UX Operations Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced planning for a feature or product area.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a UI/UX operations plan for this feature. Include design principles, component strategy, accessibility checks, responsive review, interaction states, and UIUX.md documentation tasks.

output_examples.input: Review a checkout page before implementation.
output: Confirm the primary purchase path is visually dominant and easy to scan.
Check form labels, validation messages, keyboard flow, and error recovery.
Document component choices, spacing rules, and loading states in UIUX.md.

output_examples.input: Plan a reusable profile card component.
output: Define required content, optional metadata, empty states, and responsive behavior.
Align color, typography, and spacing with existing design-system rules.
Record the component decision and usage guidance in UIUX.md.
Reduce navigation depth where possible and group related controls clearly.
Verify touch targets, readable text, feedback states, and error explanations.
Add design notes for any new patterns introduced by the flow.

best_practices: Start each review from the user goal and the task the screen must support.
Check consistency across components, colors, typography, spacing, and interaction states.
Record important design decisions and UI changes in UIUX.md.
anti_patterns: Using one-off components when an existing design-system pattern fits the need.
Shipping UI changes without checking accessibility, responsiveness, and error states.
Leaving design decisions undocumented after changing user-facing behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps review UI/UX work for consistency, accessibility, responsive behavior, interaction quality, and documentation discipline.

faq.question: Does it generate finished visual designs?
faq.answer: No. It provides operational guidance and review criteria, not final mockups or visual assets.

faq.question: Can it replace a design system?
faq.answer: No. It helps apply design-system thinking, but the project still needs defined components, tokens, and standards.

faq.question: Does it perform automated accessibility testing?
faq.answer: No. It supports checklist-based accessibility review and should be paired with automated and manual testing.

faq.question: Why does it mention UIUX.md?
faq.answer: The skill asks users to keep design specifications, decisions, and UI changes documented in UIUX.md.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only documentation content and false-positive static findings.
